concept: index a string yields a 1-char string

prompt: Write `countChar(s, ch)` returning how many times the single character `ch` appears in `s`. Use a raw index loop; do not use built-in string methods.
Write `charFreq(s)` returning an object mapping each character in `s` to its count.
tests:
if (JSON.stringify(charFreq("aab")) !== JSON.stringify({ a: 2, b: 1 })) throw new Error("aab");
if (JSON.stringify(charFreq("")) !== JSON.stringify({})) throw new Error("empty");

prompt: Write `countDigits(s)` returning how many characters of `s` are digits (0-9). Classify each char by its range; do not use regex.

concept: parse digit string via char arithmetic
prompt: Write `toInt(s)` that converts a string of digit characters into its integer value. Build the number by char arithmetic (`charCodeAt`); do not use `Number`, `parseInt`, or `+s`.
Write `repeatEach(s)` returning a string where each character of `s` appears twice in place, e.g. `"ab"` -> `"aabb"`. Accumulate pieces in an array and join.
Write `keepDigits(s)` returning a string containing only the digit characters of `s`, in order. Accumulate pieces in an array and join.
Write `shout(s)` returning `s` with every lowercase letter a-z converted to uppercase, other characters unchanged. Convert with `charCodeAt`/`String.fromCharCode`; do not use `toUpperCase`.
Write `reverseStr(s)` returning the characters of `s` in opposite order. Use a descending index loop; do not use `.reverse()` or slicing.
Write `isPalindrome(s)` returning `true` if `s` reads the same forwards and backwards. Use two pointers converging from the ends; do not reverse the string.
Write `myIndexOf(s, p)` returning the index of the first occurrence of `p` in `s`, or `-1` if absent. Use a nested scan; do not use `indexOf`, `includes`, or `search`.
Write `myJoin(parts, sep)` that concatenates the array `parts` with `sep` between adjacent elements. Return `""` for an empty array. Do not use `Array.prototype.join`.
Write `mySplit(s, sep)` that splits `s` on the single-character `sep`, returning an array of fields (empty fields included). Do not use `String.prototype.split`.
Write `roundTrip(s, sep)` returning `myJoin(mySplit(s, sep), sep)`. For a single-character `sep` this must return `s` unchanged.
